title: Adjective Endings
description: Learn the basic endings for adjectives when they are in front of nouns.
explanation:
**HOOK**
In German, adjectives like to change their 'clothes' (endings) depending on what noun they are with. It's like an adjective matching its outfit to the Gender and Case of the noun! For A1, we only focus on the most common endings.

**CONCEPT**
When an adjective is placed DIRECTLY before a noun (e.g. 'the green car'), it must take an ending. 

**RULE**
If there is a definite article (der/die/das) in Nominativ, the ending is almost always **-e** for singular! 
Example: **Das neuE Auto.**

tables.title: Nominative Adjective Endings (with der/die/das)
tables.note: TIP: Plural always takes -en! Singular takes -e with 'the' in Nominativ.

deep_notes.title: WATCH OUT
deep_notes.text: Adjective endings change in Akkusativ and Dativ, but for A1, master the Nominativ '-e' first. It covers 70% of basic descriptions!

exercises.question: Welche Endung bekommt ein Adjektiv im Plural mit 'die'?
exercises.explanation: Plural adjectives always take -en with articles!

exercises.instruction: Listen and select the correct ending you hear:
exercises.explanation: After 'ein' (masculine), the adjective takes the -er ending: guter!
